title: Cardio — Analysis
text:
Cohorts


Show cohort filters
echo_cohort <- master %>%
  filter(
    echo_ef_notavailable___999      == 0 |
    echo_late_notavailable___999    == 0 |
    echo_gls_notavailable___999     == 0 |
    echo_earatio_notavailable___999 == 0 |
    echo_eeratio_notavailable___999 == 0
  )

preterli_echo <- echo_cohort %>%
  filter(days_to_echo < days_to_terli_initiation_day0)

cat("Echo cohort N      :", nrow(echo_cohort),    "\n")


Echo cohort N      : 243 


Show cohort filters
cat("Pre-terli cohort N :", nrow(preterli_echo), "\n")


Pre-terli cohort N : 94 




Tasks 1–3 — Echo Timing Counts


Show timing-count code
timing_counts <- echo_cohort %>%
  mutate(timing = case_when(
    days_to_echo <  days_to_terli_initiation_day0 ~ "1. echo before terli",
    days_to_echo == days_to_terli_initiation_day0 ~ "2. echo same day as terli",
    days_to_echo >  days_to_terli_initiation_day0 ~ "3. echo after terli",
    TRUE                                          ~ "missing"
  )) %>%
  count(timing, name = "N")

knitr::kable(timing_counts,
             caption = "Patients in echo cohort by echo timing relative to terlipressin initiation")

Task 4 — Demographics × hrs_responders


Show demographic variable lists + Table 1 call
demographic_categorical <- c(
  "sex_male", "race", "hispanic",
  "cirrhosis_etiology", "etoh_hepatitis",
  "hegrade_admit", "admit_ascites",
  "htn_medical_hx", "dm_medical_hx", "ckd_medical_hx", "cad_medical_hx",
  "sbp_ppx_preadmission", "lactulose_preadmission",
  "ascites_previous_comp", "refascites_previous_comp",
  "he_previous_comp", "sbp_previous_comp", "nonsbp_previous_comp",
  "phtn_bleed_previous_comp", "hcc_previous_comp", "tips_previous_comp",
  "baseline_scr_outpt", "aki_previous_90days", "transfer_aki",
  "aki_precipitant_nsbpinf",
  "he_aki", "rrt_aki", "mv_aki", "pressors_aki",
  "chest_imaging", "chest_imaging_pulmedema", "chest_imaging_effusion",
  "ecg", "echo",
  "albumin_before_terli", "crystalloid_before_terli",
  "nsbb_before_terli", "mido_before_terli", "oct_before_terli", "ne_before_terli",
  "gihep_consult", "neph_consult", "pall_consult",
  "code_status",
  "ua_hrs", "ua_na_hrs", "ua_cr_hrs", "ua_urea_hrs",
  "ua_granularcasts_hrs", "ua_epi_cells_hrs",
  "location_terli_day0", "terli_route_day0",
  "tele_terli_day0", "he_terli_day0",
  "infection_terli_day0", "typeinfection_terli_day0",
  "aclf_grade", "akin"
)
demographic_numerical <- c("age")

demographic_categorical <- intersect(demographic_categorical, names(preterli_echo))
demographic_numerical   <- intersect(demographic_numerical,   names(preterli_echo))
demographic_all <- c(demographic_numerical, demographic_categorical)

dat4 <- preterli_echo %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(demographic_categorical), as.factor),
         across(all_of(demographic_numerical),   as.numeric))

t4 <- create_table_one(
  all_vars = demographic_all,
  cat_vars = demographic_categorical,
  data     = dat4,
  strata   = "hrs_responders",
  output   = "tables/task4_demographics_hrs_responders.csv"
)
knitr::kable(t4, caption = "Task 4 — Demographics by `hrs_responders` (3-level)")

📥 Download CSV — Task 4






Warningecho_gls quirk



In the pre-terli echo cohort, echo_gls ranges from −20 to +25 — some centers store GLS as a negative number (the standard convention) and others store the absolute value. 83 % of the cohort is missing GLS. Tasks 5–7 below report raw values only; medians are unstable and any future modeling on GLS should harmonize sign per center or work with abs(echo_gls).




Task 5 — Echo × hrs_responders_cat_2


Show Task 5 Table 1 call
echo_continuous <- c("echo_ef", "echo_earatio", "echo_eeratio",
                     "echo_gls", "echo_lateral_e")

dat5 <- preterli_echo %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(echo_continuous), as.numeric))

t5 <- create_table_one(
  all_vars = echo_continuous,
  cat_vars = NULL,
  data     = dat5,
  strata   = "hrs_responders_cat_2",
  output   = "tables/task5_echo_hrs_responders_cat_2.csv"
)
knitr::kable(t5, caption = "Task 5 — Echo by HRS response (binary)")

Task 8 — Cubic-Spline Logistic Regression
Outcome: hrs_responders_cat_2 (binary). Covariate: echo_ef modelled as a natural cubic spline with 3 degrees of freedom.


Show spline model fit
dat8 <- preterli_echo %>%
  mutate(
    hrs_responders_cat_2 = as.numeric(as.character(hrs_responders_cat_2)),
    echo_ef              = as.numeric(echo_ef)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(echo_ef, hrs_responders_cat_2)

fit8 <- glm(
  hrs_responders_cat_2 ~ ns(echo_ef, df = 3),
  data   = dat8,
  family = binomial
)

summary(fit8)



Call:
glm(formula = hrs_responders_cat_2 ~ ns(echo_ef, df = 3), family = binomial, 
    data = dat8)

Coefficients:
                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
(Intercept)            0.5946     1.2359   0.481   0.6304  
ns(echo_ef, df = 3)1  -1.6719     0.9641  -1.734   0.0829 .
ns(echo_ef, df = 3)2   0.7076     2.7964   0.253   0.8002  
ns(echo_ef, df = 3)3  -0.4915     1.1178  -0.440   0.6601  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 123.03  on 93  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 118.14  on 90  degrees of freedom
AIC: 126.14

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4




Show predicted-probability plot
grid8 <- tibble(
  echo_ef = seq(min(dat8$echo_ef, na.rm = TRUE),
                max(dat8$echo_ef, na.rm = TRUE),
                length.out = 200)
)

pred8 <- predict(fit8, newdata = grid8, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)
grid8 <- grid8 %>%
  mutate(
    fit   = plogis(pred8$fit),
    lower = plogis(pred8$fit - 1.96 * pred8$se.fit),
    upper = plogis(pred8$fit + 1.96 * pred8$se.fit)
  )

ggplot(grid8, aes(echo_ef, fit)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#2c8dbb") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, color = "#2c8dbb") +
  geom_rug(data = dat8, aes(x = echo_ef), inherit.aes = FALSE,
           alpha = 0.3, sides = "b") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Ejection fraction (%)",
    y = "Probability of HRS response (hrs_responders_cat_2 = 1)",
    title = "Predicted probability of HRS response by EF (natural cubic spline, df = 3)",
    caption = "Shaded ribbon = 95% CI; rug = observed EF values"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

text:
Outcome Variables

HRS Response








Variable
Description
Logic




lowest_creatinine
Lowest SCr around discontinuation
min(last_scr_2, last_scr, scr_day1_post_terli)

SCr on discontinuation day
scr_terli_day{Discont_Day}


last_scr_2
SCr day before discontinuation
scr_terli_day{Discont_Day - 1}


HRS_reversal_new
Primary outcome
lowest_creatinine <= 1.5 AND (scr_day0 - lowest_creatinine) >= 0.3


hrs_responders
Response category
0 = No stage improvement, 1 = Partial (improved but SCr > 0.3 above baseline), 2 = Complete (≤ 0.3 above baseline)


hrs_responders_cat_2
Binary responder
0 = Non-responder, 1 = Any response


hrs_rebound
SCr rebound post-terli
scr_day1_post_terli - last_scr >= 0.3





Survival / Competing Risk

Intermediate Variables








Variable
Description
Logic




dc_death_status
In-hospital death
1 if disposition == 5, else 0


after_discharge_90days_death_status
Died within 90 days post-discharge
1 if dc_death_90days == 1, else 0


time_to_death
Days from terlipressin initiation to death or censoring
In-hospital death: days_to_discharge; post-discharge death: days_to_discharge + dc_days_to_death; lost to follow-up: days_to_discharge + days_to_lost_to_followup; alive at 90 days: days_to_discharge + 90 (intentional censoring). All minus days_to_terli_initiation_day0


time_to_death_status
Overall death indicator
1 = died (in-hospital or post-discharge within 90 days), 0 = alive or lost to follow-up





90-Day Death Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day death analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death, 90) – all follow-up capped at 90 days


time_to_death_status_90days
90-day death event indicator
1 = died within 90 days; 0 = alive at 90 days, OR died after 90 days (censored at 90), OR lost to follow-up
1 only if time_to_death_status == 1 AND time_to_death <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Transplant Outcomes









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_transplant_90days
Follow-up time for 90-day transplant analysis (days)
0–90
min(time_to_transplant_cmp, 90)


time_to_transplant_status_90days
90-day transplant event indicator
2 = transplanted within 90 days; 0 = not transplanted within 90 days, OR transplanted after 90 days (censored at 90)
2 only if transplant_lt_status == 2 AND time_to_transplant_cmp <= 90; all other patients get 0





90-Day Competing Risk (Death vs. Transplant)









Variable
Description
Values
Logic




time_to_death_90days_cmp
Competing risk follow-up time (days)
0–90
min(time_to_death_90days, time_to_transplant_90days) – whichever event came first


time_to_death_status_90days_cmp
Competing risk event indicator
0 = censored (alive, no transplant within 90 days); 1 = death within 90 days (death came first or tied); 2 = transplant within 90 days (transplant came first)
If death time ≤ transplant time: use death status; if transplant time < death time: use transplant status









NoteHow the competing risk status works






Patient dies day 30, no transplant: time = 30, status = 1 (death)
Patient transplanted day 20, alive at 90 days: time = 20, status = 2 (transplant)
Patient alive at 90 days, no transplant: time = 90, status = 0 (censored)
Patient dies day 50, transplanted day 40: time = 40, status = 2 (transplant came first)
Patient died after 90 days: time = 90, status = 0 (censored – death beyond window)
Patient lost to follow-up day 60: time = 60, status = 0 (censored)







Transplant








Variable
Description
Logic




transplant_lt
LT timing category
1 = During hospitalization (LT or SLKT), 2 = Within 90 days post-discharge, 3 = After 90 days (within 1 year), 0 = No transplant


transplant_slkt
Simultaneous liver-kidney transplant indicator
1 if slkt_during_hospital == 1 OR dc_lt_90days == 2 OR lt_type_1yr == 2, else 0


transplant_lt_status
Transplant status for competing risk
0 = no transplant, 2 = transplanted (coded as 2 for competing risk with death = 1)


time_to_transplant
Days from terlipressin initiation to transplant
NA if no transplant


time_to_transplant_cmp
Transplant time for competing risk (999 if no transplant)
999 serves as a large censoring time so pmin with death time selects death


transplant_outcome
Transplanted within 90 days (binary)
1 if time_to_transplant_status_90days == 2, else 0


listed_transplant
Listed for liver transplant
1 if lt_eval == 2 OR lteval_listingstatus == 1, else 0

title: Derived Variables
text:
Multiple Imputation (revision 0311)
Predictive mean matching (PMM), m=10 imputations, 50 iterations, seed=12345.
Imputed predictors: sex_male, scr_admit, tbili_admit, sodium_admit, inr_admit, albumin_admit, bili_terli_day0, he_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0, map_terli_day0_time0, fio2_terli_day0, spo2_terli_day0, scr_terli_day0, wbc_terli_day0
Post-imputation: Clinical scores (CLIF-C, ACLF grade, Child-Pugh, MELD) are recomputed on each imputed dataset.
Models fit: Fine-Gray competing risk models pooled with Rubin’s rules via run_pooled_fg(), stratified by HRS criteria, transplant listing status, and other subgroups.

echo_earatio_notavailable___999 — Mitral E/A ratio
echo_eeratio_notavailable___999 — E/e′ ratio

Pre-terli echo cohort — echo cohort AND days_to_echo < days_to_terli_initiation_day0. All Table 1s and the spline model use this cohort.
